preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) into the voiced velar stop [g] is a form of fortition and hardening, notably documented in historical sound laws where glides transition into stops.

examples.note: Verschärfung (sharpening) where doubled glides fortified into stops in the North and East Germanic branches (e.g., 'egg').
Regular phonetic realization in Cairene Arabic where the historical palatal segment is realized as a hard velar stop.
